entry.title: Cloud ERP Close-Up: Transforming Finance-Led ERP and Service-Centric Industries with Cloud ERP Solutions
entry.content:
Welcome to a new edition of the Cloud ERP Close-Up after a short summer break!

As business complexity and pace of change rises, companies must drive rapid transformation of finance operations, monitor the business, and reduce organizational risk. Particularly fast-growing organizations have the need to follow a “post-modern ERP strategy” – which means that they differentiate between their operational and their administrative ERP capabilities.

In this blog post, we will explore how ERP solutions are revolutionizing and propelling these industries toward sustainable success. The entire blog series is all about sharing knowledge with a broader community, building confidence in the journey to the public cloud, and making product adoption a successful and delightful experience.

Last time, we put our lens on product-centric cloud ERP and additionally shared a podcast interview with Axel Herrmann, Product Management Lead for Product-Centric Cloud ERP. If you missed it, you can find it here.

This blog post will focus on Cloud ERP for finance-led and service-centric companies. I had the pleasure to interview SAP’s SVP and Head of Product for Finance-Led & Service-Centric Cloud ERP, Sabrina Kunze, and talk with her about her vision and strategy for companies in this area. But before we dive right into this interesting interview, take a closer look at the benefits of a cloud ERP for finance-led and service-centric companies:

Understanding Finance-Led ERP and Service-Centric Industries


Organizations look for a stable and reliable administrative core with high integration capabilities that support fast growth at predictable cost. At the same time, service-centric industries, such as professional services for example, are faced with downward pressure on fees, increased regulatory scrutiny, new service delivery models, lack of resources, and intense competition. Those are accelerating the pace of business while their profitability is attacked. Many companies’ organizations are aiming to move their back-office and core processes to the cloud to best leverage recent AI capabilities and achieve the next level of process automatization and effectiveness.

According to Gartner, a service-centric cloud ERP solution is a suite of integrated products that provide functionalities for at least three of the following areas: financial management, order to cash (O2C), human capital management, procure to pay (P2P), and administrative ERP.

Alongside those functional requirements, Gartner defines cloud ERP solutions for service-centric enterprises as serving companies in the professional services, software & IT, financial services, media, public services, non-profit, telecom, healthcare, and real estate markets.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition is recognized as a leader in the 2023 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Cloud ERP for Service-Centric Enterprises. Download the report here.


Figure 1: SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition is recognized as a leader in the 2023 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Cloud ERP for Service-Centric Enterprises.


Service-centric industries are at the forefront of the changing cloud ERP business landscape, facing distinct operational needs and challenges. ERP solutions tailored to these industries empower organizations to excel by streamlining processes, delivering exceptional customer experiences, and facilitating efficient financial management. By embracing cloud ERP systems, businesses can drive sustainable growth, innovation, and competitiveness in their respective fields.

Let me now welcome SAP’s SVP and Head of Product for Finance-Led & Service-Centric Cloud ERP,  Sabrina Kunze, who will share her vision and strategy for companies in this area.

Bert: Thank you for joining the Cloud ERP Close-Up, Sabrina! Let's start with your view on how the direction for SAP’s cloud ERP offering has evolved. What is crucial looking at service-centric customers’ needs?

Sabrina: Bert, thank you for having me! Across industries we see our customers exposed to a lot of change and the need to be well set with their IT to run their business. We are here to support customers solving their day-to-day business challenges and equip them best to steer their company. Especially for service-centric businesses, the solution is very well equipped to serve their growing business demands with predefined best practices across their enterprise. Comprehensive financial steering and insight to action as well as intelligent process automation is key. We utilize our deep experience in running global business processes across industries to serve our customers with our public cloud ERP solution.

Bert: What is your personal vision for service-centric industries in cloud ERP?

Sabrina: My vision is to provide our customers with a predefined public cloud ERP that allows them to smoothly and provenly adapt innovative business models, is easy to consume, and enjoyable to use. With that, it delivers value to our customers’ business users every day. Manifold business models offered as end-to-end scenarios support our customers in serving their customers best. These scenarios are designed to be easy and stepwise adoptable, supporting a transformation to a public cloud solution suite.


Figure 2: A business model for every customer need.


Bert: Why is end-to-end so important in public cloud in your eyes?

Sabrina: When our customers subscribe to a public cloud solution, they expect pre-defined best practices which they can quickly adopt running their business processes.

"Project-based services" and "solution business" are two examples for such end-to-end processes. While project-based services support selling, delivering, billing, and accounting of customer projects, the solution business enables companies to successfully transform to selling combined solutions of product, services, projects, and subscriptions. Consequently, we structured our portfolio and product priorities including the way we operate along end-to-end scenarios.

For SAP as a software company, this means the entire process flow needs to be seamlessly integrated not only for subprocesses such as creating and managing quotes, orders, contracts, or projects, but also for processes spanning logistics execution, billing, and finance. The way we offer end-to-end processes needs to support our customers’ transition to new business models to outperform their competition. We also put an emphasis on personas involved in an end-to-end scenario. After all, it’s important for everyone to have a consistent and delightful user experience while getting their job done.

An end-to-end process is composed in a modular way to utilize the best technological capabilities for each business discipline (highly elastic, scalable, deeply integrated, etc.) Therefore, our end-to-end business process offering spans from the SAP S/4HANA core to the BTP (Business Technology Platform) and integrates with our SAP Cloud portfolio. Those integrated end-to-end processes also have to fulfill cloud qualities and need to e.g., be extensible, so that partners and customers can adjust process flows as required for their specific and differentiating business environment.


Figure 3: Customer value delivered by an evolving ERP.


Bert: Let's put the lens on a few elements of quote-to-cash processes. What are our customers looking for and why?

Sabrina: Quote to cash is a key business process for many companies as it enables front and back-office operations to realize the company's top line. It provides the ability to quickly monetize new offerings, from modelling and bundling offers to managing sales quotes and orders. Further on, quote to cash handles billing, invoicing, collections, revenue recognition, and accompanying analytics. For companies operating with lean teams, having a streamlined and efficient quote-to-cash process is especially critical to their success and growth.


Figure 4: SAP solutions for quote-to-cash management.


Customers and partners have told us that, as they add subscriptions and usage-based plans, their existing quote-to-cash process is unable to accommodate these new recurring revenue models. For example, when products, services, and subscriptions are bundled together, orders end up being processed manually, leading to fulfillment delays and errors. Lack of billing transparency and inaccuracies lead to increased billing disputes. In other words, customer experience suffers, while more internal resources are spent on the quote-to-cash process.

This is why SAP is providing modular quote-to-cash solutions that allow companies to automate and streamline the entire quote-to-cash process regardless of whether they are selling physical or intangible products or services on a subscription base, along with usage plans, or any other business model. This trend towards growing flexibility of the offering requires agile adoptable process support. Daily operations must be executed with a significantly higher degree of automation and benefit from embedding artificial intelligence in the quote-to-cash process chain. This helps customers to reduce operational costs by achieving a higher level of operational excellence at the same time.

Consequently, we are continuously investing in new innovations that leverage AI and machine-learning capabilities. Some examples are identification of non-paying customers, optimization of customer payment to balance out cashflow as well as automated creation of sales orders finally evolving to a low touch, intelligent order management.

Excited to take a deeper look and connect? Find more insights here: CPQ, Billing, Accounts Receivable, Revenue Management.

Bert: As you just mentioned, one aspect all our readers are interested in: The whole world is talking about AI. What are your thoughts on SAP’s opportunities with AI?

Sabrina: Look, we need to distinguish between the most recent discussions around generative AI and what I would call traditional AI cases. Within Cloud ERP, we have started offering AI cases and closely engage with our customers and product management on an impactful AI offering early on. Two prominent examples are our cash application software and our offering to create sales orders via automatic extraction:

https://support.sap.com/content/dam/productfeatures/assets/00033/901B0ED1A0641EDAB8BDCC9CB0A1C0F7/demos/Sales_Order_Automation_Video.mp4

Figure 5: This video shows the creation of sales orders via automatic extraction.


Most of the traditional AI scenarios target efficiency and cost savings, while the next generation of AI unleashes a new potential. The way generative AI and large language models take presence in our day-to-day life requires all of us to shift perspective. It offers a huge potential for unlocking new creativity and productivity, given we use it in a responsible way. By embedding generative AI into our business applications, we apply this, framed by the ethical and business standards we stand for, while unlocking new ways of interacting with the software and prepare better business decisions and value creation. Please also see:

  • SAP Business AI,

  • Business AI in Finance,

  • 3 Ways CFOs Can Capitalize on AI in Finance 


Thank you so much for your time and the great insights, Sabrina!

 



 

Doubling down on Sabrina’s words on order automation, especially efficient sales order processing is a critical aspect of any business, and in today’s rapidly changing business landscape, organizations are constantly seeking ways to improve efficiency and enhance customer satisfaction. SAP S/4HANA Cloud supports your order-to-cash processes with low touch order management and automated billing capabilities. Internal sales representatives can make better decisions with actionable insights that make solving problems seamless.

It is important to ensure that orders be filled correctly before they are shipped. SAP is working to speed up the processing of customer orders by prepopulating details from your master data and based on historic entries and auto-completing missing information using AI and machine learning technologies.

If you are interested in more of the existing AI use cases concerning the intelligent sales order creation and execution powered by AI, see the following blog post:









 

Sales in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition – Automated Order Process with SAP's Intelligent Technologies

I hope you enjoyed our conversation and can make the best use of the shared content.

I hope to see you as a frequent follower of our Cloud ERP Close-Up – stay tuned and discover more product highlights next month.

All the best,

Bert

Follow us on @SAP and #S4HANA, and myself on bert.schulze, LinkedIn or Twitter.

 

Inside SAP S/4HANA Podcast


As always, I would like to encourage you to subscribe to our podcast “Inside SAP S/4HANA”. This podcast leverages the unique knowledge and expertise of SAP S/4HANA product experts, partners and customers to address objects of interest by sharing product insights and project best practice. There is no customer success without product success and project success; we share all ingredients with you to get to the next level and make your SAP S/4HANA project a success. Subscribe now and benefit from the shared knowledge.


 

openSAP microlearnings for SAP S/4HANA


Our openSAP microlearnings for SAP S/4HANA offer an exciting new learning opportunity. What began with a small batch of 20 videos, has now become a channel with more than 50 microlearnings that have generated over 20,000 views. Today we cover multiple lines of business such as finance, manufacturing, and supply chain, and key technology topics like Master Data Management, extensibility, SAP User Experience, and upgrade management. We are continuously adding new microlearnings to the SAP S/4HANA channel, so make sure you check them out.


 

Your Voice Matters!


If you want to learn more and actively engage with SAP subject matter experts on SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition, join our SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition Community – now fully integrated with SAP Community. The community brings together customers, partners, and SAP experts and has a clear mission: deliver an interactive community to engage with one another about best practices and product solutions. We invite you to explore the SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition Community ‘one-stop shop’ as the central place for all resources, tools, content questions, answers and connect with experts to guide you through your journey to SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition.


 

More Information:



  • Highlights of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition 2308 release

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition 2308 intelligent ERP update

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition release info

  • Release blog posts

  • YouTube playlist for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition

  • YouTube playlist for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition and SAP S/4HANA

  • SAP S/4HANA PSCC Digital Enablement Wheel

  • Best practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition Community

  • Feature Scope Description

  • What’s New

  • Help Portal Product Page

entry.title: Build Relevant, Reliable and Responsible AI for Business at SAP TechEd 2023
entry.content:
Remember how the internet literally changed everything in the early ‘90s? I still remember the unmistakable chirp of my first modem slowly connecting to some great unknown – the worldwide web! I’d wait patiently for the famous voice of AOL to tell me, “You’ve got mail!”  

Believe it or not, artificial intelligence is disrupting industries in the same way today – quite literally revolutionizing the way we interact and do business. 

What do both have in common? People. 

Whether you’re a developer or not, AI has democratized access to information at a large scale.  

It is reshaping a world where enterprise systems discover unexpected opportunities to improve business process efficiency while reducing carbon footprints, where we can focus on our biggest challenges without wasting time on tedious and repetitive tasks. 

With SAP Business AI, we have already entered this exciting future thanks to our deep business process knowledge. Built on 50 years of experience generating 87% of the world’s commerce, SAP delivers a unique focus on integration, data quality and trust that our customers have come to expect.  

The fact is: more than 26,000 customers of cloud solutions from SAP now use SAP Business AI across multiple scenarios and partner solutions. 

At SAP TechEd this November, we’re thrilled to reconnect and get you up to speed with SAP Business AI with insights from SAP experts, customers and partners. 

You can join us:  

  • in-person at SAP TechEd Bangalore, to connect directly with SAP experts, industry influencers, and our vast developer and IT practitioner community or, 
  • online at SAP TechEd Virtual, our interactive event including live and on-demand educational sessions as well as an engaging Q&A. 

At both events, our dedicated AI track will be divided into two sub-tracks, offering sessions in lecture or interactive formats such as keynotes or jump-start sessions, to deliver the learning experience that works best for you. 

Within these sub-tracks, you’ll learn how SAP delivers Business AI that is relevant and responsible, taking enterprise software into the generative AI age and supporting our customers along their own AI transformation journey. 

Subtrack #1: AI Built for Business 
What is in it for you? 

  1. Unlock valuable insights and make agile business decisions with AI built into your SAP applications. 
  2. Learn how to responsibly accelerate development with AI models pretrained on business-relevant data. 
  3. Discover ways to deploy and run AI models at scale and manage the entire AI lifecycle in one central place. 

Subtrack #2: Generative AI and Large Language Models 
What is in it for you? 

  1. Discover how to responsibly combine the power of generative AI with the context of your business data for tailored business results in your SAP applications.  
  2. Explore how you can develop and extend applications using generative AI.  
  3. Learn about SAP's generative AI strategy and how this powerful technology can help you remain agile. 

After just two days at TechEd, you'll leave with the skills, solutions, and confidence you need to stay relevant and ahead of the curve in the fast-paced AI environment.  

Join us in Bangalore or online at the AI track! We can’t wait to learn, share, and grow with all of you. Your AI ideas get real at SAP TechEd.  

Register now

See you in November!

In the meantime, visit our AI/ML topic page on the SAP Community for additional learning resources and join our new community group to interact with AI enthusiasts.

entry.title: Learn about Localization at SAP TechEd 2023!
entry.content:
SAP TechEd 2023 is just around the corner, and we are thrilled to invite you to a series of curated sessions that will delve deep into the fascinating topic of localization. Get ready to expand your knowledge, network with SAP experts, and gain valuable insights into the latest updates for localization.

Check out the list of sessions we have curated for you:

Go Global Confidently with Localized SAP Solutions and SAP BTP (Strategy Talk –  ST103)


Friday, November 03 - 11:00 a.m. - 11:45 a.m. IST


Learn from customer examples how enterprises operate globally through localized SAP solutions and SAP Business Technology Platform, addressing requirements such as security, integration, artificial intelligence, app development, and extensibility. See how companies meet regulatory requirements for e-invoicing, statutory reporting, and business-to-government interactions using the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance solution integrated with SAP S/4HANA and go global confidently.



 

Tax Check Automation Using Machine Learning (Mini-Theater Session – AI540)


Friday, November 03 – 03:00 p.m. - 03:45 p.m. IST


Tax checks were required for businesses to extend audit automation to United Kingdom, Australian, and New Zealand markets. Automating tax checks can reduce auditing efforts and enable faster reimbursements. Learn how multimodal transformers use machine learning models that make use of both image and text-based information to identify tax-related information from receipts. You will get insights from SAP experts in data curation, annotation, fine-tuning, and service-deployment pipelines.



 

Empowering SAP Customers to Go Global Confidently - Localization of SAP S/4HANA Cloud


Demo booth - open during the event


SAP customers are empowered to operate globally through SAP’s localized solutions. Stop buy our booth and learn how localization is delivered by SAP including, “localization as a self-service” which provides low-code/no-code extensibility capabilities. Experience real world localization scenarios for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, and SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, SAP’s solution for statutory reporting and electronic invoicing for automated compliance. Get ready for how SAP Localization can propel your business around the world and into the future.



You can find the complete session catalog here.

Do not miss this opportunity to connect and learn more about SAP’s localization offerings.

We are looking forward to meeting you at SAP TechEd 2023!

entry.title: Fairness in Machine Learning - A New Feature in SAP HANA PAL
entry.content:
In this blog post, we are excited to introduce Fairness in Machine Learning (FAIR ML) - a new feature developed to promote fairness in machine learning within the SAP HANA Predictive Analysis Library (PAL) in 2023 QRC04.

Fairness is a critical subject within human society. The pursuit of fairness is essential in many social areas involving resource allocation, such as college admission, job candidate selection and personal credit evaluation. In such areas when human decision gets involved, it is important to ensure that the decision-makers must be endowed with a sense of fairness to avoid giving undue advantage to any particular group. Fortunately, an increasing number of decision-makers are reflecting this sentiment of fairness, signaling an encouraging trend.

As our society progresses, its complexity is also increasing. As a result, many automated decision-making systems, powered by machine learning and AI, have been developed. These systems come initially just as helpers or aiding components, yet there is a clear trend that they are began to play more significant roles. For instance, many HR implemented systems for automated filtering of job applicants in recent years. In the US, a software called PredPol (short for "Predictive Policing") estimates potential crime rates across different regions based on historical data. A tool named COMPAS is employed by US courts to assess the probability of a defendant becoming a repeat offender.

However, one of the major concerns with these automated decision-making systems is their lack of fairness awareness. Unlike many human decision-makers, the majority of these systems are constructed without a conscious ethos of fairness or rely on limited, usually inadequate, fairness-aware mechanisms. For example, a study shows that PredPol could potentially lead to over-policing towards Black communities, while COMPAS trends to misclassify Black defendants as having a high risk of reoffending compared with other racial groups. It is noted that, as claimed by their creators, sensitive variables such as racial groups are never explicitly incorporated when building up the two aforementioned systems. This reveals that bias is not always easily removable through data preprocessing methods (like feature selection), since it may hide deeply within those seemingly uncorrelated features for sensitive variables that are inevitable model training (e.g. crime history in COMPASS).

For SAP, AI fairness is one of the three pillars of SAP Global AI ethics policy and a fundamental principle of AI ethics which aims to prevent discrimination by AI systems against specific demographic groups based on protected attributes like gender, ethnicity, or religion. Given that many of our products, including SAP S/4 HANA, utilize machine learning algorithms for predictive analysis, it is of utmost importance that our algorithms are fairness-aware when needed.

FAIR ML within the SAP HANA Predictive Analysis Library (PAL) strives to mitigate the unfairness resulting from potential biases within datasets related to features such as gender, race, age or other protected classes. FAIR ML can incorporate various machine learning models or technologies, which adds to its versatility. Currently, we support binary classification and regression tasks  Hybrid Gradient Boosting(HGBT) as a sub-model.

In the following sections, let's take a publicly accessible “Give me some credit” dataset as a case study to explore the fairness-related harms introduced in the machine learning model, and then how to mitigate such bias with our new feature of FAIR ML.

Dataset Description


“Give me some credit”  dataset, comprising 150,000 rows and 12 columns contains sample data where the target variable named ‘SeriousDlqin2yrs’ indicates that a borrower experiencing financial distress in the next two years. This is a typical application of binary classification where the output could be ‘1’ (will experience financial distress) or ‘0’ (will not experience financial distress).

The remaining 11 columns consist of an ID column and various financial related data of a borrower such as age, monthly income, debt ratio, and the number of real estate loans, etc. Using this data, we can construct a predictive model to calculate an individual’s likelihood of encountering a financial crisis in the next two years.

Data Pre-processing


For the purpose of this study, data preprocessing for our example involved simplifying the ‘age’ column into two employment status groups (‘working’ and ‘retired’) by introducing a new proxy feature called ‘working_status’ based on an age threshold of 60. Ages over 60 are classified as ‘retired,’ and those under or equal to 60 are categorized as ‘working’. Following this, the ‘age’ column was removed. This case study will concentrate on the sensitive variable ‘working_status’.

Continuing further, our underlying assumption is that if a bank anticipates a financial crisis impacting the borrower within the next two years, it will opt to deny a loan to mitigate potential default. To align with this, we rename our target variable “SeriousDlqin2yrs” to “default”, facilitating a shift in our problem statement. Now, our focus is on predicting whether a borrower is more likely to default. To represent this, we still use ‘1’ and ‘0’, where ‘1’ signifies potential default and ‘0’ indicates the borrower will not default.

Additional data pre-processing steps include:

    • Handling missing values : The ‘MonthlyIncome’ column is filled with a mean value for missing values, and the ‘NumberOfDependents’ column is filled with a median value for missing values.
    • Outlier detection : Outliers detected in columns ‘NumberOfTime30-59DaysPastDueNotWorse’, ‘NumberOfTimes90DaysLate’, ‘NumberOfTime60-89DaysPastDueNotWorse’ are replaced with the median.
    • Dataset split : The dataset is partitioned with 80% (120,000 data points) as a training dataset for model training and the remaining 20% (30,000 data points) as a testing data for model validation.
    • Imbalanced dataset handling : The ratio of label 0 and label 1 is approximately 14:1, which means most data instances belong to the borrower who will not experience financial distress in the next two years. To balance the training data, a balanced dataset with a 1:1 ratio has been sampled from the original data set with regard to the label ‘SeriousDlqin2yrs’.


Training an unmitigated classifier

In this section, we will train a fairness-unaware model on the training data with a sub-model of the Hybrid Gradient Boosting(HGBT) Classifier  with hyperparameter tuning and grid search.

from hana_ml.algorithms.pal.unified_classification import UnifiedClassification

from hana_ml.algorithms.pal.model_selection import GridSearchCV



# train the model with grid search
hgc = UnifiedClassification('HybridGradientBoostingTree')
gscv = GridSearchCV(estimator=hgc, 
                   param_grid={'learning_rate': [0.1, 0.4, 0.7],
                   'n_estimators': [50, 100],
                   'max_depth': [5, 10, 15],
                   'split_threshold': [0.1, 0.4, 0.7]},
                   train_control=dict(fold_num=5,
                                      resampling_method='cv',
                                      random_state=1,
                                       ref_metric=['auc']),
                                       scoring='error_rate')

gscv.fit(data=df_train, 
         key= 'ID',
         label='default',
         partition_method='stratified',
         partition_random_state=1,
         stratified_column='default',
         build_report=True)
# predict
pred_result = gscv.predict(data=df_test.deselect('default'), key='ID')


The model report is shown as follow.

 

Fig 1. Model Report of a HGBT model

Upon evaluation, the metrics in Fig. 1 such as precision, recall, F1 score for two classes, the Area Under the Curve (AUC), and accuracy appear satisfactory. We will proceed to assess the model using an imbalanced testing dataset. In this scenario, our primary sensitive variable is ‘working_status’. Our goal is to determine whether our classification model can fairly predict defaults, irrespective of the borrower’s working status - whether they are retired or currently employed.

When discussing fairness in AI systems, the first step is to understand the potential harms that the system may cause and how to identify them. In our example, if the decision to grant a loan is based on predicting default, a harmful situation occurs when the model incorrectly predicts that a borrower who will not default, will default. This can lead to the bank denying the loan based on model bias, despite the borrower being low-risk. Consequently, the model may cause harm by misallocating resources.

To assess this situation, we can observe the metric called “The False Positive Rate (FPR)”. The FPR is calculated by dividing the number of false positives (incorrectly predicted defaults) by the total number of real negative cases (borrowers who will not default). It represents the proportion of false alarms generated by the model. A high FPR indicates that the model produces many false alarms, meaning it predicts that many borrowers will default on their loans when they will actually pay on time.

The charts below present the accuracy and FPR for different employment status groups in predicting the testing data.

 

Fig 2. Accuracy and FPR of a Unmitigated HGBT Model

 

Table 1. Accuracy and FPR of Two Groups


In the accuracy chart in Fig. 2, we can observe a higher value for the retired group (0.91) compared to the working group (0.74). However, there is a notable disparity in the False Positive Rate (FPR) between the two groups in Fig. 2. The FPR value for the retired group is approximately 7.8%, while it’s approximately 26% for the working group. Hence, we believe that the model might inaccurately predict that working individuals will default. Consequently, the system could unfairly allocate fewer loans to working individuals and over-allocate loans to retired individuals.

Unfairness Mitigation in ML Models


In this section, we employ FAIR ML to mitigate the unfairness in the HGBT classifier. The key concept is to achieve a randomized classifier with the lowest error while satisfying the desired constraints. For binary classification, various constraint options are available such as: “demographic_parity”, “equalized_odds”, “true_positive_rate_parity”, “false_positive_rate_parity”, and “error_rate_parity”. For regression tasks, an option “bounded_group_loss” is supported. The selection of constraints depends on the inherent bias of the problem.

In our case study, as FPR result in fairness-related harms. Hence, we will attempt to minimize the FPR with a constraint option "false_positive_rate_parity".

from hana_ml.algorithms.pal.fair_ml import FairMLClassification



hgbt_fm = FairMLClassification(fair_constraint='false_positive_rate_parity')

hgbt_fm.fit(data=df_train, key='ID', label='default', 

            fair_sensitive_variable='working_status', 

            fair_positive_label ='1',

            categorical_variable=['working_status', 'default'])



pred_res_fm = hgbt_fm.predict(data=df_test.deselect('default'), key='ID')


The figures below show the comparison results of accuracy and FPR between the original HGBT model and the mitigated HGBT model for two groups.

Fig 3. Accuracy of Original and Mitigated Models

 

Fig 4. FPR of Original and Mitigated Models

Table 2. Accuracy and FPR of Two Groups of a Mitigated Model

As evident from the above results in Fig.3 and Fig.4, the fairness-aware model significantly lowers the FPR disparity between the two groups. There is an increase in the false positive rate for the retired group, coupled with a decrease for the working group. However, this correction introduces a trade-off that results in the accuracy of the retired group falling from 91% to 80%.

Summary

In this blog post, we first discuss the importance of fairness in AI, particularly in machine-learning decision-making systems. Even though automated systems have increased in complexity and significance, many lack robust fairness-aware mechanisms and inadvertently allow biases to persist. Then, a case study of with the “Give me some credit” dataset where unfairness associated with a sensitive attribute is detected and mitigated using Fair ML of SAP HANA PAL. The example underscores the need for fairness mitigation in machine learning models to decrease disparities, avoid harm, and ensure fairness in decision-making. Predictive models must not only be accurate and efficient, but also equitable to all demographic segments, underlining the essential role of fairness in machine learning.
 

Other Useful Links:

hana-ml on Pypi.

We also provide a R API for SAP HANA PAL called hana.ml.r, please refer to more information on the documentation.

For other blog posts on hana-ml:

  1. A Multivariate Time Series Modeling and Forecasting Guide with Python Machine Learning Client for SAP HANA
  2. Outlier Detection using Statistical Tests in Python Machine Learning Client for SAP HANA
  3. Outlier Detection by Clustering using Python Machine Learning Client for SAP HANA
  4. Anomaly Detection in Time-Series using Seasonal Decomposition in Python Machine Learning Client for SAP HANA
  5. Outlier Detection with One-class Classification using Python Machine Learning Client for SAP HANA
  6. Learning from Labeled Anomalies for Efficient Anomaly Detection using Python Machine Learning Client for SAP HANA
  7. Python Machine Learning Client for SAP HANA
  8. Import multiple excel files into a single SAP HANA table
  9. COPD study, explanation and interpretability with Python machine learning client for SAP HANA
  10. A Multivariate Time Series Modeling and Forecasting Guide with Python Machine Learning Client for SAP HANA

entry.title: SAP CodeJam HANA ML (Duesseldorf, DE) Recap
entry.content:
This week we had the Getting Started with Machine Learning using SAP HANA and Python in Duesseldorf, Germany kindly hosted by Deloitte.

Special thanks to @Assem_Aly for all the local organizational efforts:

entry.title: Exploring the Business Benefits of AI
entry.content:
Artificial Intelligence (AI) has emerged as a transformative technology that is revolutionizing various industries. From automating routine tasks to enabling personalized customer experiences, AI is driving significant business benefits across diverse sectors.

There are numerous advantages that AI brings to businesses, highlighting its potential to enhance efficiency, productivity, decision-making, and customer satisfaction.

Enhanced efficiency and productivity

One of the key advantages of AI is its ability to automate repetitive and time-consuming tasks. By leveraging machine learning algorithms, businesses can streamline processes, reduce human error, and improve overall efficiency. AI-powered automation can handle tasks such as data entry, data analysis, customer support, and inventory management, freeing up human resources to focus on more strategic and creative endeavors. This increased efficiency leads to higher productivity, cost savings, and improved resource allocation.

Data-driven decision-making

AI has the potential to unlock valuable insights from vast amounts of data. By analyzing patterns, trends, and correlations, AI algorithms can generate actionable intelligence that aids in decision-making. Businesses can use AI to optimize pricing strategies, forecast demand, identify market trends, and personalize marketing campaigns. With AI-powered analytics, organizations can make data-driven decisions that are more accurate, timely, and aligned with their business goals.

Improved customer experience

AI technologies, such as natural language processing and machine learning, have revolutionized customer service. Chatbots and virtual assistants can provide instant and personalized responses to customer queries, improving response times and enhancing overall customer experience. AI-powered recommendation systems can analyze customer preferences and behaviors to offer personalized product recommendations, leading to increased customer satisfaction and loyalty. Moreover, AI can help businesses gain a deeper understanding of their customers through sentiment analysis, social media monitoring, and customer segmentation, enabling targeted marketing and better customer relationship management.

Enhanced cybersecurity

As businesses increasingly rely on digital infrastructure, cybersecurity has become a critical concern. AI can play a pivotal role in safeguarding sensitive data and detecting potential threats. Machine learning algorithms can analyze network traffic patterns, identify anomalies, and proactively respond to security breaches. AI-powered systems can also continuously learn and adapt to new threats, making them an invaluable asset in the fight against cybercrime.

Streamlined operations and supply chain management

AI can optimize various aspects of operations and supply chain management. Predictive analytics can forecast demand, allowing businesses to optimize inventory levels and reduce wastage. AI-powered algorithms can also optimize production schedules, logistics routes, and delivery processes, leading to cost savings and improved efficiency. By harnessing AI, businesses can streamline their operations, minimize downtime, and ensure timely delivery, thereby gaining a competitive edge.

The business benefits of AI are vast and diverse. From enhancing efficiency and productivity to enabling data-driven decision-making and improving customer experiences, AI is transforming the way businesses operate. By embracing AI technologies, organizations can gain a competitive advantage, drive innovation, and unlock new opportunities for growth. As AI continues to evolve, it is crucial for businesses to explore and harness its potential to stay ahead in today's dynamic and competitive marketplace.

SAP gives customers over 130 AI capabilities embedded into SAP applications, empowering their business processes with AI that's as intuitive as it is flexible and powerful. Tailored to our customers’ unique data landscape and industry nuances, our AI enables smarter decisions and efficiencies at scale, while our unwavering commitment to responsibility ensures trust and compliance in every interaction.

Interesting in learning more?

  • Read about SAP’s Business AI strategy
  • Meet Joule, SAP’s AI co-pilot
  • What is generative AI?

entry.title: Artificial Intelligence and Technologies in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402
entry.content:
Welcome to my blog post on Artificial Intelligence and Technologies. With the start of this year, I have started to cover latest updates from not only AI but also the technologies that lay the foundation for AI and enable SAP systems to be smart. This update will cover the latest from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402.

Let's get started with my YouTube video below on the quick highlights.

Video: Discover what's new in Artificial Intelligence and Technologies

Here are the highlights:

  • Introduction to SAP Business AI Approach
  • Generative AI at SAP
  • Introducing Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Updates from Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management (ISLM)
  • Custom Configuration of Review Booklets with Review Booklet Designer

SAP Business AI Approach

SAP Business AI refers to a suite of artificial intelligence (AI) solutions and capabilities developed by SAP, a leading provider of enterprise software solutions. SAP Business AI leverages AI technologies to enhance various aspects of business operations, including decision-making, process automation, customer engagement, and data analysis. SAP's AI foundation is laid on SAP Business Technology Platform.

Figure: SAP Business AI Approach

Key components and functionalities of SAP Business AI include:

  1. Intelligent Automation: SAP Business AI offers tools for automating repetitive tasks and business processes through AI-powered robotics process automation (RPA) and machine learning algorithms. This streamlines operations, improves efficiency, and reduces manual effort.

  2. Predictive Analytics: SAP Business AI provides predictive analytics capabilities that enable organizations to forecast future trends, identify patterns, and make data-driven decisions. This helps businesses anticipate market changes, optimize resource allocation, and mitigate risks.

  3. Conversational AI (Digital Assistant): SAP Business AI includes conversational AI solutions, such as Joule, that facilitate natural language interactions between users and enterprise systems. These conversational interfaces enhance user experience, and enable self-service functionalities.

  4. Intelligent Insights: Through advanced analytics and AI algorithms, SAP Business AI generates actionable insights from large volumes of data, enabling businesses to uncover hidden trends, opportunities, and correlations. This empowers organizations to make informed decisions and drive business growth.

  5. Personalized Customer Engagement: SAP Business AI enables personalized customer engagement by analyzing customer data and behavior to deliver targeted recommendations, offers, and experiences. This enhances customer satisfaction, loyalty, and retention.

  6. AI-powered Applications: SAP integrates AI capabilities into its enterprise applications, such as SAP S/4HANA Cloud, to enhance their functionality and performance. This enables users to leverage AI-driven features seamlessly within their existing workflows.

Overall, SAP Business AI represents a comprehensive suite of AI-driven solutions designed to help organizations optimize their business processes, drive innovation, and achieve their strategic objectives in an increasingly digital and data-driven world.

For more information, please visit SAP Business AI

Generative AI at SAP

In today's rapidly evolving digital landscape, businesses are constantly seeking innovative ways to leverage technology for competitive advantage. At SAP, we understand the importance of staying ahead of the curve, which is why I'm excited to introduce you to our Generative AI approach.

Foundation Models

SAP's Foundational Models are not confined to text alone; they encompass speech, images, and structured data, offering a wide array of capabilities. From summarization and writing assistance to question answering and code generation, these models are designed to revolutionize the way businesses operate.

Tailored Solutions for Your Business Needs

What sets SAP's Foundational Models apart is our commitment to customization and personalization. We understand that every business is unique, which is why we tailor industry knowledge and generic results to meet your specific needs. Through prompt engineering and embedding, we ensure that these models are seamlessly integrated into your existing workflows, providing precise and specific solutions for your business challenges.

Figure: SAP's Approach to Generative AI

Real-World Applications

The applications of SAP's Generative AI are limitless. From delivering intelligent and collaborative ERP solutions to interacting with Cloud ERP systems in natural language, these technologies empower businesses to streamline operations, access information, and solve complex tasks with ease. Whether it's improving the accuracy of document extraction, assisting in content creation and knowledge management, or boosting development productivity with code generation.

Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is introduced with release 2402 in Early Adopter Care Program. More details below.

Introducing Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Starting SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 release, Joule is now available in the Early Adopter Care Program (EAC)*. This AI copilot is designed to revolutionize your interaction with SAP business systems. At its core, Joule aims to simplify every touchpoint and streamline tasks, ultimately enhancing your business operations.

Understanding Joule:

Joule is a comprehensive solution tailored to understand the intricacies of your business. It acts as your companion in navigating the complex SAP Ecosystem, offering transparent, role-based access to relevant processes from any location. With Joule, you experience a unified and delightful user interface across SAP's solution portfolio.

Key Features and Capabilities:

Joule facilitates conversational interactions, enabling seamless communication between humans and computers. It supports three primary conversational patterns:

  1. Navigational: Helping users find the functionality they need within SAP systems.
  2. Transactional: Assisting users in efficiently completing tasks.
  3. Informational: Retrieving relevant information from existing documents.

Moreover, Joule operates within various SAP BTP environments, including Cloud Foundry, Kyma, Kubernetes, or others. It is a multitenant application, ensuring data isolation and independent provisioning for different consumers.

Have a look at the short demo video below.

Demo: Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (EAC)

Enterprise-Grade Integration:

Joule seamlessly integrates with SAP applications, offering a rich web client compliant with SAP Fiori UI controls. Its out-of-the-box integration with SAP backend systems ensures enterprise readiness while maintaining compliance with AI ethics, GDPR, privacy controls, and SOC-II compliance standards.

Business Benefits:

The benefits of Joule are manifold:

  • Faster Work: Streamline tasks with an AI assistant that understands your unique role and serves as your copilot across SAP applications.
  • Smarter Insights: Gain quick answers and intelligent insights on-demand, facilitating faster decision-making.
  • Better Outcomes: Access excellent content for job descriptions, coding assistance, and more with just a simple query.
  • Full Control: Retain full control over decision-making and data privacy while leveraging generative AI in a secure environment.

*Availability with Early Adopter Care Program (EAC)

Joule is available in the Early Adopter Care program and more information can be found on the influence platform.

  • Registration Start:* January 9, 2024
  • Release to Customer:* March 11, 2024
  • Registration End:* June 28, 2024

Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management (ISLM)

Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management (ISLM) is a framework that allows you to perform lifecycle management operations on machine learning scenarios. You can consider ISLM as a self-service tool that allows you to handle various operation requirements of machine learning scenarios. You can train an intelligent scenario and use the trained model to get an accurate inference result.

More information on ISLM is available on SAP Help Portal.

I have two updates to share from ISLM.

Sales Order Auto Completion Use case

In the fast-paced world of business, efficiency and accuracy are paramount. That's why we are thrilled to introduce our latest ISLM use case: Sales Order Auto Completion. Leveraging cutting-edge machine learning technology within the Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management (ISLM) framework on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), this new scenario is set to transform the way sales orders are processed.

Introducing Sales Order Auto Completion

With Sales Order Auto Completion, users are empowered to streamline the sales order creation process like never before. By harnessing the power of Data Attribute Recommendation, this scenario provides intelligent recommendations derived from historical data, enabling users to complete sales orders with unparalleled ease and precision.

Key Features and Benefits

  • Efficiency: Say goodbye to manual data entry and time-consuming processes. Sales Order Auto Completion significantly reduces manual efforts, allowing users to complete sales orders in a fraction of the time.
  • Accuracy: With intelligent recommendations at their fingertips, users can rest assured that their sales orders are accurate and complete, minimizing errors and mitigating delays.
  • Integrated Data Management: Training the machine learning model is made easy with integrated data management handled via ISLM. This seamless integration ensures that users have access to the most relevant and up-to-date data for training purposes.

How It Works

Sales Order Auto Completion seamlessly integrates with the SAP Fiori app "Manage Sales Orders - Version 2", providing users with recommendations for input fields that are crucial for further processing. Additionally, the ISLM App Intelligent Scenarios Management enables users to train the model, upload data for training, view model metrics, and activate it for productive usage.

Watch the demo video below.

Demo: AI-Based Sales Order Autocompletion with SAP S/4HANA Cloud

Availability

Ready to experience the future of sales order management? Sales Order Auto Completion is now available with SAP S/4HANA CFD 2308.3. Increase efficiency, enhance accuracy, and revolutionize your sales order processes with this game-changing solution.

Conclusion

In conclusion, Sales Order Auto Completion represents a positive shift in sales order management. By harnessing the power of machine learning and intelligent recommendations, businesses can streamline processes, reduce manual efforts, and drive greater efficiency and accuracy. Embrace the future of sales order management with Sales Order Auto Completion today.

Looking for more details? This innovation is covered in the blog post by Christian Vogler, Sales in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 

Multi Model Management

In today's dynamic business landscape, adaptability and flexibility are key to staying ahead. That's why I am excited to announce this latest feature within Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management (ISLM): Multi Model Management. This latest enhancement empowers users to activate multiple concurrent models based on specific business contexts, revolutionizing the way models are managed and deployed.

Introducing Multi Model Management

With Multi Model Management, ISLM users can now activate various model versions simultaneously, each tailored to different business contexts. This capability opens up a world of possibilities, allowing organizations to align their AI models with specific segments and fields, thereby maximizing their relevance and effectiveness. The screenshot below shows the Multi model check box.

Image: Multi Model Management in ISLM

Key Benefits and Value Proposition

  • Enhanced Flexibility: Gone are the days of one-size-fits-all model deployment. With Multi Model Management, organizations can activate different model versions based on values for primary segment fields and segment fields, ensuring that each model is finely tuned to its respective business context.
  • Visibility and Control: Gain insight into the activation context for each model, providing greater transparency and control over the model deployment process.

Custom Configuration of Review Booklets with Review Booklet Designer

* Note: the following is section is taken from the blog post Finance in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 by Ulrich Hauke where it is covered in greater depth.

Using the Review Booklet Designer, analytics key users can now create custom review booklets. They use this new functionality in the Manage KPIs and Reports app to copy the SAP-delivered review booklet (Group Financial Statements Review Booklet) and adapt it to your reporting needs. In this process, you connect the content of an analytical query with measures, structures, and dimensions and display the results on a business page of the review booklet.

Value Proposition 

  • Create customer-specific reporting experiences and improve the adaptability of reporting content making use of a richer feature set.

Review Booklet Designer

Capabilities 

  • Use the analytical queries that are part of the standard review booklet
    • Define the content of business pages with rows, columns, and filters
    • Define variables and default values
    • Configure semantic styles and navigation targets

For more information, see: Review Booklet Designer, Creating a Review Booklet in the Review Booklet Designer

-----------------------------------

Further Information and Links to Useful Resources

Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 Early Release Series!

In January 2024, we hosted a series of 19 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 release. Missed the live sessions? No problem! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you on demand.

Inside SAP S/4HANA Cloud

There is no customer success without project success and product success! Leverage the unique knowledge and expertise of SAP S/4HANA Cloud experts, partners, and customers, sharing their SAP S/4HANA Cloud implementation project best practices and lessons learned – anywhere, anytime. In this podcast, we give you all ingredients to get to the next level and make your SAP S/4HANA Cloud project a success. Subscribe now and benefit from the shared knowledge!

openSAP Microlearnings for SAP S/4HANA Cloud

Our openSAP microlearnings for SAP S/4HANA Cloud offer an exciting new learning opportunity. What began with a small batch of 20 videos, has now become a channel with more than 50 microlearnings that have generated over 20,000 views. Today, we cover multiple lines of business such as finance, manufacturing, and supply chain management, and key technology topics like Master Data Management, extensibility, SAP User Experience, and upgrade management. We are continuously adding new microlearnings to our channel, so make sure you check them out.

Your Voice Matters

You want to learn more and actively engage with SAP subject matter experts on SAP S/4HANA Cloud topics? We have just the right place for you! Join our interactive community where we bring together customers, partners, and SAP experts with a clear mission: To engage with one another about best practices and product solutions. We invite you to explore the ‘one-stop shop’ as the central place for all resources, tools, content questions, answers and to connect with experts who will guide you through your journey to the intelligent, sustainable enterprise.

Find All of Our Assets

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

Where to Find More Information:

  • Watch our openSAP microlearnings to learn about SAP S/4HANA Cloud topics at your own pace
